config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to zip a file or directory and create a zip with the name you specify.

args.description: Filepath of the directory or file to zip up.
args.name: source_file_path

args.description: Filepath of the output zip file. Eg. C:\Users\Admin\Desktop\image.jpg
args.name: destination_zip_file_path

config.description: This script enables a specified Windows optional feature using PowerShell. You can control whether the system restarts after enabling the feature by setting the 'restart' parameter.

args.description: The Windows feature name to enable.
args.name: feature

args.description: Set to 'true' to restart after enabling, 'false' to not restart.
args.name: restart

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to list out the execution context for the given process ID. This lists out a variety of information including the current working directory of the process, the command line and the user associated with the process.

args.description: Process ID of the process to list the execution context for.
args.name: processId

data.title: List Execution Context for the Given Process ID

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to list out available memory info for the given process ID.

args.description: Process ID of the process to list memory details for.
args.name: processId

config.description:
This script detects potentially sideloaded DLLs on Windows endpoints. It enumerates DLLs 
currently loaded by running processes, and reports any DLL whose load path does not match 
the expected legitimate locations.

args.description: Process name to inspect (without .exe). Use 'all' to scan every running process.
args.name: process_name

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to list out the parent and child processes for the given process ID. This will effectively generate a miniature process tree for the provided process ID.

args.description: Process ID of the process to list parent and child processes for.
args.name: processId

data.title: List the Parent and Child Processes for the Given Process ID

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows. It can cleanup the WMI persistence from the host. Orbital queries can help to get the event filter and consumer names to pass to this script as parameters necessary for cleanup actions. Please read each parameter's description for the specific query names.

args.description: WMI event filter name. Orbital's WMI Event Filters Monitoring query can be used to get the WMI event filter name.
args.name: event_filter_name

args.description: WMI event consumer name. Orbital's WMI Command Line Event Consumer Monitoring query can be used to get the WMI event consumer name.
args.name: event_consumer

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows. It identifies services that match the provided full name (case-insensitive), then stops and deletes the service.

args.description: Full name of the Windows service to stop and delete. Case-insensitive.
args.name: service_name

config.description: This script queries a system to list all installed programs, by detecting OS type. It is compatible with windows, linux and mac operating systems.
This script is applicable to Windows. It can execute a powershell cmdlet on the host.

args.description: Powershell cmdlet to execute.
args.name: cmdlet

config.description:
This script combines detection and remediation for CVE-2026-31431 on Linux endpoints. 
It first determines if the system is vulnerable by checking the algif_aead module availability,
load state, modprobe blocking rules, and kernel patch status. If vulnerable, it automatically
applies mitigation by creating a modprobe install override and unloading the module from memory. 
If already mitigated or not vulnerable, no changes are made.
This script reads and displays the current DNS configuration from the endpoint. It automatically detects the operating system and retrieve DNS servers and search domains.

This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It will try tp parse the provided microcode_amd_fam17h.bin container file to find the patch for CVE-2023-20593 (Zenbleed). If patch is not found in the container file, the script will print Vulnerable.

args.description: The full path of the microcode container file (microcode_amd_fam17h.bin). In Unix like systems it is usually located in /lib/firmware/amd-ucode/microcode_amd_fam17h.bin
args.name: amd_ucode_container_file_path

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. For mitre test.
This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will print a test string for the no of times as provided in the parameter.

args.description: No of times to print test string
args.name: no_of_lines_to_print

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It will show pid, process_name and username (tab separated) respectively.

args.description: Process name to show.
args.name: process_name

args.description: Show all processes opened by a specific user.
args.name: user_name

config.description: Disables the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) feature on Windows. Optionally restarts the computer based on the 'restart' parameter.

args.description: Set to true to restart after disabling WSL, false to not restart.
args.name: restart

data.title: Disable Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) Feature

config.description: This script lists the patch status of an endpoint. It is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux.
This script is applicable to MacOS and Linux. It will check Cisco Secure Endpoint Isolation status.

data.title: Check Cisco Secure Endpoint Isolation Status

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will list shared folders on the host.
This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will print a test string for the no of times as provided in the parameter.

args.description: No of seconds to wait/sleep
args.name: timeout_in_seconds

config.description: This script installs available operating system patches on Windows, macOS, and Linux endpoints. It supports installing all available patches or a specific patch/package by name.

args.description: Specific patch/package name to install, or 'all' to install all available patches.
args.name: patch_name

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows. It can set a registry value similar to RegSetValueEx Windows API.

args.description: Full path of the registry key including value to be modified. For example, if you want to modify value of target_value under Run key registry, you have to input "HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\target_value" (without quotes).
args.name: registry_key_path

args.description: New value to be set. This value MUST BE base64 encoded. For REG_BINARY data type you have to provide hex string like DEADBEEFDEADBEEF encoded in base64. For REG_DWORD, value could be in hex starting with '0x' or an integer value. For REG_SZ string encoded in base64 is accepted.
args.name: new_value

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It can unmount a drive or network share including cifs share on Linux.

args.description: The drive name like E: (with colon) or network path like /mnt/share (Linux) or \\computername\share (Windows).
args.name: drive_share_name

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to identify on a system, versions of Apache Struts that are vulnerable to CVE-2023-50164. If no parameter is specified, it automatically searches all directories on a system for any struts version on it.

args.description: Full path to a custom path to be searched.
args.name: directory_path

data.title: Apache Struts Versions Vulnerable To CVE-2023-50164

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will return MD5, SHA256, and SHA1 of a specified file.

args.description: The path to the file that will be hashed.
args.name: filename

config.description:
This script is applicable to Windows only. It queries Windows boot entries and checks whether Safe Mode (safeboot) is enabled on the specified entries. 
It will check the {current} and {default} boot entries and report their Safe Mode status.

data.title: Windows Boot Entry Safe Mode Checker

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to logout the host.

args.description: The user to logout.
args.name: user_name

config.description: This script will check the status of Windows Subsystem for Linux feature.

data.title: Check the Status of WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) Feature

config.description: It enumerates all scheduled tasks and persistence jobs configured on the endpoint, auto-detecting the OS and using the appropriate method per platform.
This script is applicable to Windows only. It will attempt to provide details for a given Windows Service.

args.description: A string to search for in the Windows Service name or display name fields. The default search is Threat Defense.
args.name: serviceName

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It will attempt to decode a base64 string and save the output to a file path of your choosing. This is a workaround to enable file upload to a system.

args.description: The base64 string representation of a file you want to upload to the endpoint.
args.name: inputString

args.description: The output file path to save the data to.
args.name: outputFilePath

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to list out as much of the relevant details for the given process ID as it can.

args.description: Process ID of the process to list the readily available details for.

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It will attempt to list out all network connections by protocol: tcp, upd, inet, unix, or all. Supplying no argument will list all network connections.

args.description: Protocols to list out network connections for.
args.name: connectionProtocol

config.description:
Captures network traffic for a specified duration and saves it as a PCAP file in the system's default temporary directory. 
The script auto-detects the OS and uses the appropiate tool to capture all interface's traffic.

args.description: Name of the output capture file (with or without extension).
args.name: filename

args.description: Duration of the capture in seconds (must be at least 1).
args.name: duration

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It's exit code will always be 2 to test partial success in UI.
This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It's a large script with only strings to print of about 253KB in size.

args.description: Set to 'true' to restart after disabling, 'false' to not restart.

args.name: startup_entry_name

config.description:
This script lists all available Windows optional features, or validates if a specific feature is available on the system.
Set the 'feature' parameter to 'all' to list, or to a feature name to validate.

args.description: Feature name to validate, or 'all' to list all available features.
args.name: feature

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will list currently running user sessions.
This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to restart the host.

Checks whether Cisco Network Visibility Module (NVM) is installed on the endpoint.
Uses a multi-source fallback detection strategy per platform: package manager,
binary on disk, service/daemon registration, and running process.
Returns module name, installation status, version, and location.

data.title: Cisco Network Visibility Module (NVM) Installation Check

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to delete a file provided in the parameters. If the "force" paramater is set to True, then the script will attempt to delete the file in the sensitive folders like System32 and Program Files in case of Windows and /Applications and other system paths in MacOS and Linux.

args.description: The absolute file path to delete.
args.name: file_path

args.description: If this flag is set to True, it can delete files in system folders. Please see description for more details.
args.name: force

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It can stop a running service provided the service name.

args.description: Service name
args.name: service_name

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It will attempt to submit the provided filepath to ThreatGrid. It requires your ThreatGrid API Key to work.

args.description: Full path of the file or folder you wish to submit to ThreatGrid
args.name: filePath

args.description: The API Key to use in order to submit the sample to ThreatGrid. This Key must match the region to which you are submitting. The API Key can be found under your account settings in the ThreatGrid Portal UI.
args.name: api_key

args.description: The region of ThreatGrid servers to submit to. By default samples are sent to our North American servers. Customers in the EU will need to specify the region as EU and ensure that they are using an API Key from the threatgrid.eu account page.
args.name: region

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It will attempt to list out all disk partitions on the machine.

This script is applicable to Windows only. It will attempt to list out all Windows Services or search for services which contain the provided search string. Supplying no argument will list all Windows Services.

args.description: A string to search for in the Windows Service name or display name fields. The default will list out all services on the system.
args.name: service_name

config.description: This script is intended to be used for downloading installers for additional products or applications but does not expressly enforce that the files are installers.

args.description: Direct URL to the Windows installer you want to download.
args.name: url

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It will attempt to base64 encode a file of your choosing and print the base64 output to you. This is a workaround to enable file download from a system.

args.description: The path of a file you want to downlod from the endpoint.
args.name: inputFilePath

data.title: Download File from Endpoint as a Base64 String

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows. It will attempt to delete an item from the autorun registry keys. This script has the ability to delete an item from multiple locations all at once.  If you just want to print the existing keys, please set "show_all_run_keys" parameter to True.

args.description: Autorun key name to delete (case sensitive). For example SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run has a key called "malware" and value "C:\temp\malicious.exe", then you have to provide "malware" in this parameter.
args.name: autorun_key_name

args.description: If this flag is set to True, it will show all the items in the run keys given in the description and will not delete the item from run keys.
args.name: show_all_run_keys

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows. It can delete a scheduled task using command "schtasks /delete /F /TN task_name".

args.description: Scheduled task name. Please note that the scheduled_task_name parameter must be the full path of the task. For example, if the task name is Firefox_updater and the directory is \Mozilla then this parameter should be \Mozilla\Firefox_updater.
args.name: scheduled_task_name

config.description:
This script identifies all listening network services on the
endpoint and classifies each by risk level based on port number,
binding interface, and known service signatures. It flags database
ports exposed externally, known backdoor ports, debug interfaces,
admin panels, and services binding to all interfaces when they
should be local-only. Output is a structured JSON report with
findings and actionable recommendations.
Generates a list of Startup Applications.
This script searches for an installed program by matching the display name. If found, executes the uninstallation silently. It is compatible with windows, linux and mac operating systems.

args.description: Exact name of the program to uninstall.
args.name: program_name

config.description:
This script is applicable to Windows. It can add mitigation for CVE-2023-36884 by adding HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Internet Explorer\\Main\\FeatureControl\\FEATURE_BLOCK_CROSS_PROTOCOL_FILE_NAVIGATION key into Windows registry with apps which will be affected by this feature. 
- Advisory link -> https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/07/11/storm-0978-attacks-reveal-financial-and-espionage-motives/

This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It can rename a directory provided in the parameters. If the "force" paramater is set to True, then the script will attempt to rename the directory located in the protected folders like System32 and Program Files in case of Windows and /Applications and other system paths in case of MacOS and Linux. This script has essentially two functions:
- Rename directory
- Move directory

args.description: Full path of the directory to be renamed.
args.name: directory

args.description: Full path of the new directory. If the path is a different directory, script will move the directory to the new location with the new name.
args.name: new_directory

args.description: If True, will rename protected directories like System directories.
args.name: force

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to list out the environment variables for the given process ID.

args.description: Process ID of the process to list the environment variables for.
args.name: processId

data.title: List Environment Variables for the Given Process ID

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS and Linux. It can list any existing network connections.

This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It will attempt to delete a directory provided in the parameters. If the "force" paramater is set to True, then the script will attempt to delete the directory located in the protected folders like System32 and Program Files in case of Windows and /Applications and other system paths in case of MacOS and Linux.

args.description: Full path of the directory to be deleted.
args.name: directory_path

args.description: If True, will delete protected directories like System directories.
args.name: force

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, MacOS, and Linux. It will attempt to shutdown the host. The Windows Shutdown/Restart History Monitoring Query can be used to verify success.

args.description: Number of seconds to wait before shutting down.
args.name: wait_seconds

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It will attempt to list a directory contents with sha256. The stdout will have file name and sha56 (tab separated). If it's not a regular file, then \"Not a regular file\" will be printed instead of sha256.

args.description: Full path of the directory to be listed.
args.name: directory_path

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, and Linux. It will list out any available thread information for the given process ID.

args.description: Process ID of the process to list thread details for.
args.name: processId

config.description: This script is applicable to Windows, Linux and MacOS. It will attempt to list a directory's content. If the "recursive" parameter is set to "True", then the script will also list sub-directories.

args.description: Full path of the directory to be listed.
args.name: directory_path

args.description: If True, will recurse through sub-directories.
args.name: recursive

config.description:
This script silently installs an executable (.exe) or MSI (.msi) installer on Windows systems.
It supports quiet mode and no reboot. Useful for remote software deployment.

args.description: Absolute path to the folder containing the installer.
args.name: path

args.description: Name of the installer file (e.g., installer.exe or package.msi).
args.name: filename

config.description: It recursively scans a given directory and returns all files whose last-modified timestamp (mtime) falls within the specified lookback window (in minutes).

args.description: Full path of the directory to scan recursively for recently modified files.
args.name: directory_path

args.description: Lookback window in minutes
args.name: minutes
